metadata.changelog: v4.0: verified all v3.0 CIDOC-CRM mappings (correct). Expanded with: volume, area, circumference in dimensional; color, texture, finish, patina, hardness, porosity in material; damage_type, fragility, completeness in state; tool_marks, joining_technique in technical; bounding_box, footprint_area in spatial; duration, terminus_post_quem, terminus_ante_quem, relative_chronology in temporal; ceremonial_function, ritual_function, defensive_function, domestic_function, symbolic_function in telic; school, workshop, period_attribution in stylistic. NEW subcategories: iconographic + semantic under cultural_interpretive (iconographic_subject, narrative_content, iconographic_program, aesthetic_value, religious_significance, monumental_status, attribution). Added provenance_history, exhibition_history, publication_history under administrative; NEW ownership subcategory (current_custodian, ownership_chain). NEW CATEGORY epistemic — meta-qualia central to EM paradata model (confidence_level, certainty_level, uncertainty_factors, methodology_used, validation_method, source_quality, primary_contributor, review_status, last_modified).

qualia.name: Origin Type
qualia.description: Natural or artificial origin

qualia.name: Color
qualia.description: Perceived color of the surface or material; classified by controlled vocabulary (Munsell, Getty AAT) or expressed as RGB/HEX value.

qualia.name: Texture
qualia.description: Tactile or visual surface quality (smooth, rough, granular, polished, etc.).

qualia.name: Surface Finish
qualia.description: Final treatment of the surface (matte, glossy, polished, brushed, etc.) distinct from surface_treatment which is the procedure.

qualia.name: Patina
qualia.description: Surface alteration layer formed naturally over time (oxidation, weathering, biofilm) or artificially. Modeled as both an observable feature (state) and the result of a modification event.

qualia.name: Hardness
qualia.description: Mechanical hardness measured on a standard scale (e.g., Mohs for minerals, Vickers for metals).

qualia.name: Porosity
qualia.description: Fraction of void volume in the material (0-100%), relevant for stone, brick, mortar.

qualia.name: Damage Type
qualia.description: Typology of observed damage (cracks, fragmentation, abrasion, erosion, missing parts, biological colonization).

qualia.name: Fragility
qualia.description: Susceptibility of the object/feature to damage; qualitative assessment by conservator.

qualia.name: Completeness
qualia.description: Percentage of the original feature/object that survives. Distinct from integrity (which is overall structural soundness): completeness measures missing material.

qualia.name: Tool Marks
qualia.description: Traces left by specific tools on the surface (chisel marks, saw cuts, hammer impacts). Captures both the type of mark and the inferred tool. Closely related to the WorkingUnit (UL) stratigraphic node.

qualia.name: Joining Technique
qualia.description: Method used to join structural or decorative elements (mortar, clamp, dowel, dovetail, tenon-and-mortise).

qualia.name: Elevation
qualia.description: Height above sea level

qualia.name: Arrangement
qualia.description: Spatial relationship with other objects

qualia.name: Bounding Box
qualia.description: Axis-aligned bounding volume of the element expressed as min/max coordinates. Useful for spatial indexing and rough collision/containment checks.

qualia.name: Footprint Area
qualia.description: Plan-view area of the element projected on the horizontal plane. Critical for architectural archaeology (rooms, structures, sites).

qualia.name: Dating Method
qualia.description: Method used for dating

qualia.name: Duration
qualia.description: Length of the time-span associated with the element (event duration, period span). Distinct from absolute_time_start/end which are anchors: duration is the magnitude.

qualia.name: Terminus Post Quem
qualia.description: Earliest possible date for the element ('not earlier than'). One of the two foundational dating bounds in archaeological chronology.

qualia.name: Terminus Ante Quem
qualia.description: Latest possible date for the element ('not later than'). The companion bound to terminus_post_quem; together they bracket the dating window.

qualia.name: Relative Chronology
qualia.description: Position of the element in a relative sequence (before/after specific reference units), independent of absolute dating. Common when absolute dates are unavailable.

qualia.name: Primary Function
qualia.description: Main intended function of the object

qualia.name: Secondary Functions
qualia.description: Additional or subsidiary functions

qualia.name: Ceremonial Function
qualia.description: Use in non-domestic, non-utilitarian ceremonies (civic processions, banquets, public rituals). Distinct from ritual_function (sacred) and symbolic_function (representational).

qualia.name: Ritual Function
qualia.description: Sacred or religious use of the element (cult activity, offering, burial). Distinct from ceremonial (civic) and symbolic (representational).

qualia.name: Defensive Function
qualia.description: Use in defense or fortification (walls, towers, gates, ditches).

qualia.name: Domestic Function
qualia.description: Use in everyday household life (storage, cooking, sleeping, washing).

qualia.name: Symbolic Function
qualia.description: Representational/symbolic role beyond practical use (status display, identity, narrative). Closely related to iconographic and aesthetic qualia.

qualia.name: Structural Role
qualia.description: Role in the overall structural system

qualia.name: Stress Type
qualia.description: Type of mechanical stress the element is subject to

qualia.name: Load Capacity
qualia.description: Maximum load the element can bear

qualia.name: Stylistic Influences
qualia.description: Cultural or artistic influences

qualia.name: School / Workshop
qualia.description: Production school or workshop attribution (e.g., 'School of Phidias', 'Della Robbia workshop').

qualia.name: Period Attribution
qualia.description: Stylistic period to which the element is attributed (e.g., 'Late Republican', 'Early Imperial'). Distinct from absolute dating: this is the stylistic classification.

iconographic.name: Iconographic Qualia
iconographic.description: Subject matter, narrative content, and iconographic program of the element. Specific to artistic or representational objects.

qualia.name: Iconographic Subject
qualia.description: Primary subject or figure depicted (deity, mythological scene, portrait, allegory).

qualia.name: Narrative Content
qualia.description: Story or scene depicted (Trojan War, biblical narrative, historical episode). Free-text or linked to a thematic concept node.

qualia.name: Iconographic Program
qualia.description: Coherent set of subjects forming a thematic whole (e.g., 'Programma augusteo del Foro di Augusto'). Higher-level than individual subjects.

semantic.name: Semantic Qualia
semantic.description: High-level interpretive qualia: aesthetic judgment, religious significance, monumental status, attribution. These are inherently propositional (model as crminf:I4_Proposition_Set in RDF, not as direct dimensions).

qualia.name: Aesthetic Value
qualia.description: Aesthetic judgment about the element ('beautiful', 'sublime', 'monumental', 'refined'). Inherently subjective; the canonical qualia for the EM 'bello/brutto' interpretive axis.

qualia.name: Religious Significance
qualia.description: Religious or sacred role attributed to the element (cult image, relic, sanctified space).

qualia.name: Monumental Status
qualia.description: Recognition of the element as a monument of cultural significance (national heritage, UNESCO, regional protected).

qualia.name: Attribution
qualia.description: Attribution to a specific maker, patron, or commission (e.g., 'attributed to Phidias', 'commissioned by Augustus'). Modeled as an attribute-assignment event in RDF to carry confidence.

qualia_categories.name: Contextual Qualia
qualia_categories.description: Experimental category for management and administrative information

qualia.name: Inventory Number
qualia.description: Official inventory or catalog number

qualia.name: Legal Status
qualia.description: Legal protection and ownership status

qualia.name: Intervention History
qualia.description: History of conservation interventions

qualia.name: Conservation Status
qualia.description: Current conservation state and needs

qualia.name: Access Conditions
qualia.description: Conditions for access and use

qualia.name: Provenance History
qualia.description: Chain of provenance events for the element: production → ownership transfers → current location. Modeled in RDF as a chain of crm:E10_Transfer_of_Custody and crm:E8_Acquisition events linked to the entity.

qualia.name: Exhibition History
qualia.description: Record of public exhibitions in which the element has appeared. Modeled in RDF as crm:E7_Activity instances with temporal scope.

qualia.name: Publication History
qualia.description: Chain of publications referencing the element. Modeled as crm:E31_Document references via P67_refers_to.

ownership.name: Ownership Qualia
ownership.description: Current and historical ownership / custody of the element. Distinct from administrative qualia which cover legal/inventory aspects: ownership is the chain of who has held the thing.

qualia.name: Current Custodian
qualia.description: Current legal custodian or owner (institution or person) of the element.

qualia.name: Ownership Chain
qualia.description: Historical sequence of custodians/owners. Modeled in RDF as a chain of crm:E8_Acquisition events ordered by time.

qualia_categories.name: Epistemic Qualia
qualia_categories.description: Meta-qualia about how knowledge of a node was constructed: confidence in the assertion, methodology followed, and attribution chain (who said what, validated by whom, when). Central to the Extended Matrix paradata model — every claim in EM carries an epistemic dimension. This category is the explicit machine-readable expression of EM's reasoning chain.

confidence.name: Confidence Qualia
confidence.description: Quantitative and qualitative confidence in assertions about the node

qualia.name: Confidence Level
qualia.description: Numerical confidence in the assertion (0-100%). The EM-canonical scalar for paradata-supported claims.

qualia.name: Certainty Level
qualia.description: Qualitative certainty discrimination, complementary to confidence_level for non-numerical reasoning.

qualia.name: Uncertainty Factors
qualia.description: Factors that reduce certainty about the assertion (partial preservation, missing documentation, conflicting sources).

methodology.name: Methodology Qualia
methodology.description: Methods and procedures used to construct the assertion

qualia.name: Methodology Used
qualia.description: Method or procedure followed to derive the assertion (analytical, comparative, experimental, etc.).

qualia.name: Validation Method
qualia.description: Method used to validate the assertion (peer-review, cross-validation, replication, etc.).

qualia.name: Source Quality
qualia.description: Quality assessment of the sources supporting the assertion.

attribution_chain.name: Attribution Chain Qualia
attribution_chain.description: Provenance of the assertion: who made it, who validated it, when

qualia.name: Primary Contributor
qualia.description: Author/agent (human or AI) who first asserted the claim. Links to AuthorNode (human) or AuthorAINode (AI subclass) via has_author edge.

qualia.name: Review Status
qualia.description: Current review status of the assertion in the paradata workflow.

qualia.name: Last Modified
qualia.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assertion.
